user_query:
I’m organizing materials for my Modern Poetry seminar and want to standardize my notes and produce a concise announcement for students. Please use the provided files to build two structured outputs and one edited message.

Inputs:
- Poems data: input/library/poems.csv
- Visiting poets HTML snapshot: input/web/schedule.html
- Theme taxonomy: config/taxonomy.yaml
- Reference code to inspect: scripts/build_index.py
- Draft announcement: input/drafts/announcement.md

Requirements:
1) Inspect scripts/build_index.py and use the SCHEMA constant as the single source of truth for exact field names and column order. Do not invent or rename fields.
2) Create outputs/poets.json as a list of objects, one per unique author in input/library/poems.csv. For each author:
   - poem_count: number of poems in the CSV by that author
   - appears_in_schedule: true/false based on whether the author’s name appears in input/web/schedule.html under the visiting-poets list
   - first_appearance_date: the earliest ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) from the <time datetime="…"> tag for that author in the HTML, or null if absent
   - sample_titles: up to 3 of that author’s poem titles from the CSV, sorted alphabetically
   Sort the list by author ascending. All field names must match SCHEMA["poets_json_fields"].
3) Create outputs/poems_by_theme.csv with one row per (poem, theme) pair. Split the themes column by semicolons, map each tag through config/taxonomy.yaml alias_to_canonical. Include only rows where a canonical mapping exists. Columns must match SCHEMA["poems_by_theme_fields"]. Sort rows by author, then year, then title, then canonical_theme.
4) Rewrite input/drafts/announcement.md to outputs/announcement_edited.md (120–160 words). Keep a clear, welcoming, authoritative tone. Preserve the exact meeting time and location as stated in the draft. Include a sentence noting that the compiled indexes are available at the paths: outputs/poets.json and outputs/poems_by_theme.csv. Avoid filler, hedging, and administrative digressions.

Verification notes:
- The sum of poem_count across outputs/poets.json must equal the number of rows in input/library/poems.csv.
- Authors marked appears_in_schedule=true must have a corresponding entry in input/web/schedule.html with a <time> tag; first_appearance_date must match the earliest such datetime.
- Every canonical_theme in outputs/poems_by_theme.csv must appear as a value in alias_to_canonical.

Deliverables:
- outputs/poets.json
- outputs/poems_by_theme.csv
- outputs/announcement_edited.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
